base_rate_info: 1 in 1000 people in the population have Disease X. A test for Disease X is 99% accurate (99% true positive rate, 99% true negative rate).
observation: A randomly selected person tests positive for Disease X.
target_event: the person actually has Disease X

base_rate_info: Only 0.1% of email is genuine phishing. A spam filter flags emails as phishing with 95% accuracy for actual phishing and a 5% false positive rate.
observation: The spam filter flags an incoming email as phishing.
target_event: the email is genuinely a phishing attempt

base_rate_info: In a city, 95% of taxis are Blue and 5% are Green. A witness reports the taxi in an accident was Green. Witnesses correctly identify cab color 80% of the time.
observation: The witness says the cab was Green.
target_event: the cab involved in the accident was actually Green

base_rate_info: Drug-impaired driving causes 1% of all accidents. A roadside test detects impairment with 90% sensitivity and has a 10% false positive rate on sober drivers.
observation: A driver fails the roadside impairment test.

base_rate_info: 1 in 10,000 chips manufactured by a plant is defective. An automated inspector catches 99% of defective chips but incorrectly flags 0.5% of good chips.
observation: The inspector flags a chip as defective.
